english: Fish Burger with Spicy Tomato Sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard spicy tomato fish burger weighing approximately 160 g, consisting of a deep-fried battered fish fillet, spicy tomato sauce, lettuce, and mayonnaise in a wheat bun.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Fish Burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Fish Burger (cr_id G00010109). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Spicy Tomato Fish Burger. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the dominant carbohydrate component being a refined white wheat bun, analogous to white bread GI ~70–75 per University of Sydney GI Database. The battered fish fillet contributes additional refined carbohydrate from coating. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 29.57 × 1.6) / 100 ≈ 33.1 for a 160 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content at 487 mg/100g with low potassium-to-sodium ratio (197 mg K vs 487 mg Na per 100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger due to its high sodium load of 487 mg/100g (approximately 779 mg per 160 g serving); request reduced or no spicy tomato sauce and mayonnaise to lower sodium intake, and pair with high-potassium foods to partially offset the sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate content (29.57 g/100g) from white wheat bun and battered fish coating, with low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g) and estimated high GI (~70)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this burger because the refined white bun and battered fish coating produce a high glycaemic load (~33 per serving), causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation; if consumed, reduce portion size, remove the bun, and choose a grilled fish alternative to lower the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium (487 mg/100g), trans fat (0.1 g/100g from deep-frying), saturated fat (~2.1 g/100g), and cholesterol (29 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this burger due to its atherogenic nutrient profile — high sodium elevates blood pressure, trans fat from deep-frying raises LDL and lowers HDL cholesterol, and the overall fat composition is unfavourable; opting for a grilled fish version with reduced sauce would substantially lower cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat present (~0.1 g/100g); tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this burger as deep-frying destroys the anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids in the fish and generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation; trans fat at any level is a hard disqualifier for RA management, and the tomato-based sauce (nightshade) should be monitored for individual tolerance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Fish Burger is a Western-style fast-food sandwich popular in Singapore, consisting of a deep-fried battered white fish fillet served in a soft refined wheat bun with spicy tomato sauce, lettuce, and mayonnaise. The fish fillet is coated in a seasoned batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy, giving it a crunchy exterior and moist, flaky interior. The spicy tomato sauce provides a tangy, mildly hot flavour profile that distinguishes it from a classic fish burger. Per 100g, the burger delivers 263 kcal, 13.3g protein, 10.18g fat, and 29.57g carbohydrate, with 487mg sodium — a notably high sodium level. The refined bun and battered coating contribute a high glycaemic index (~70) and low dietary fibre (1.8g/100g), making this a high-glycaemic-load meal. Clinically, this burger is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium, poor K:Na ratio), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI, low fibre, high refined carbohydrate), cardiovascular disease (trans fat, saturated fat, high sodium), or rheumatoid arthritis (AGEs from deep-frying, trans fat, nightshade sauce). It is a comfort food best consumed occasionally and in moderation, with modifications such as removing the bun or requesting grilled fish to improve its nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Spicy Tomato Fish Burger (cr_id G00010109) with a stated confidence of 0.95. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread as the dominant carbohydrate reference, which is a well-established and defensible approach.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific HPB Spicy Tomato Fish Burger entry is not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on the input health_suitability section which noted approximately 0.1g/100g trans fat from deep-frying; this is not explicitly reported in the HPB per_100g table and should be verified against the full HPB source record. saturated_fat_g estimated at 2.1g/100g based on total fat of 10.18g and typical saturated fat proportion (~21%) for deep-fried battered fish products per USDA FoodData Central reference (Fish fillet, battered or breaded, fried, USDA SR Legacy); this field is not explicitly reported in the HPB record. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item as of 2026-04-16. GI estimated at 70 (high) by analogy with white bread from the University of Sydney GI Database, as no direct GI measurement for Spicy Tomato Fish Burger exists in approved sources. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore fast-food fish burger preparation; kosher status not confirmed. is_dairy_free set to true as no dairy ingredients are listed; mayonnaise is typically egg-based without dairy in Singapore fast-food context.
